description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Michigan (  MISH-ig-ən) is a peninsular state in the Great Lakes region of the Upper Midwestern United States.

description: city in Marathon County, Wisconsin, United States of America
description_wiki: Schofield is a city in Marathon County, Wisconsin, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Marathon County, Wisconsin, United States
description_wiki: Wausau (  WAH-sah or Wə-sə) is a city in Marathon County, Wisconsin, United States, and its county seat.

description: region across the Alps
description_wiki: Tyrol ( tih-ROHL, ty-ROHL, TY-rohl; historically the Tyrole; German: Tirol [tiˈroːl] ; Italian: Tirolo) is a historical region in the Alps of Northern Italy and western Austria.

description: Italian city in the North-East of the country
description_wiki: Trento, also known in English as Trent,  is a city on the Adige River in Trentino-Alto Adige/Südtirol in Italy.

description: air warfare branch of the U.S. Army from 1926 to 1947
description_wiki: The United States Army Air Corps (USAAC) was the aerial warfare service component of the United States Army between 1926 and 1941.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Fort Sheridan may refer to:

surface_forms: December 7, 1941, and hearing about Pearl Harbor
description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: United States Army installation located in North Central Kansas
description_wiki: Fort Riley is a United States Army installation located in North Central Kansas, on the Kansas River, also known as the Kaw, between Junction City and Manhattan.

description: United States Army cavalry regiment
description_wiki: The 7th Cavalry Regiment is a United States Army cavalry regiment formed in 1866.

description: United States cavalry commander (1839–1876)
description_wiki: George Armstrong Custer (December 5, 1839 – June 25, 1876) was a United States Army officer and cavalry commander in the American Civil War and the American Indian Wars.

description: family name
California Historic Landmark
description_wiki: Camp Ibis was one of twelve divisional tent camps of the US Army Desert Training Center (DTC) which was established in early 1942 during World War II, originally to advance desert tank warfare and to train troops for desert combat.

description: city in San Bernardino County, California, United States
description_wiki: Needles is a city in San Bernardino County, in the Mojave Desert region of Southern California.

description: bend or curve in a coastline, river, or other geographical feature
United States Army installation in Vernon Parish, Louisiana, United States, formerly named Fort Johnson
description_wiki: Fort Polk, formerly Fort Johnson, is a United States Army installation located in Vernon Parish, Louisiana, about 10 miles (15 km) east of Leesville and 30 miles (50 km) north of DeRidder in Beauregard Parish.

description: U.S. military facility in New Jersey
description_wiki: Camp Kilmer is a former United States Army camp in Central New Jersey that was activated in June 1942 as a staging area and part of an installation of the New York Port of Embarkation.

description: retired British ocean liner
description_wiki: RMS Queen Mary is a historic retired British ocean liner that operated primarily on the North Atlantic Ocean from 1936 to 1967 for the Cunard Line.

description: town and administrative centre of Inverclyde, Scotland, UK
description_wiki: Greenock ( ; Scots: Greenock; Scottish Gaelic: Grianaig, pronounced [ˈkɾʲiənɛkʲ]) is a town in Inverclyde, Scotland, located in the west central Lowlands of Scotland.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: Scotland is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: military installation at Tidworth in Wiltshire, England
description_wiki: Tidworth Camp is a military installation at Tidworth in Wiltshire, England.

description: 1944 code name for the right flank of the Allied landing beaches in Normandy
description_wiki: Utah, commonly known as Utah Beach, was the code name for one of the five sectors of the Allied invasion of German-occupied France in the Normandy landings on June 6, 1944 (D-Day), during World War II. The westernmost of the five code-named landing beaches in Normandy, Utah is on the Cotentin Peninsula, west of the mouths of the Douve and Vire rivers.

description_wiki: Sainte-Marie-du-Mont may refer to the following communes in France:

description: 1916 battle on the Western Front during the First World War
description_wiki: The Battle of Verdun (French: Bataille de Verdun [bataj də vɛʁdœ̃]; German: Schlacht um Verdun [ʃlaxt ʔʊm ˈvɛɐ̯dœ̃]) was fought from 21 February to 18 December 1916 on the Western Front in France.

description: commune in Luxembourg
description_wiki: Mersch (French pronunciation: [mɛʁʃ], German pronunciation: [mɛʁʃ]; Luxembourgish: Miersch [ˈmiːɐ̯ʃ] ) is a commune and town in central Luxembourg, situated in the canton of the same name.

formation of the United States Army (1918/19 and since 1932)
country in Western Europe
description_wiki: Luxembourg,  officially the Grand Duchy of Luxembourg, is a landlocked country in Western Europe.

description: country in western Europe
description_wiki: Belgium, officially the Kingdom of Belgium, is a country in Northwestern Europe.

description: town in the commune of Consdorf, in eastern Luxembourg
description_wiki: Scheidgen (Luxembourgish: Scheedgen) is a town in the commune of Consdorf, in eastern Luxembourg.

description: village
description_wiki: Medernach (Luxembourgish: Miedernach) is a town and former commune in eastern Luxembourg.

description: village in Luxembourg
description_wiki: Ermsdorf (German pronunciation: [ˈɛʁmsˌdɔʁf]; Luxembourgish: Iermsdref) is a village and former commune in eastern Luxembourg.

description: village in Luxembourg
description_wiki: Eppeldorf (Luxembourgish: Eppelduerf) is a village in the commune of Ärenzdallgemeng, in eastern Luxembourg between Bettendorf and Beaufort.

description: 1942-1945 United States Army formation
description_wiki:
The 9th Armored Division (the "Phantom Division") was an armored division of the United States Army during World War II. In honor of their World War II service, the 9th was officially nicknamed the "Phantom Division."
The 9th Armored Division was cited for extraordinary heroism and gallantry in combat in the vicinity of Waldbillig and Savelborn, Luxembourg from 16 to 22 December 1944 during which they repulsed constant and determined attacks by an entire German division.

description: arm of the Atlantic Ocean that separates southern England from northern France
description_wiki: The English Channel, also known as the Channel, is an arm of the Atlantic Ocean that separates Southern England from northern France.

description: municipality in the province of Antwerp, Belgium
description_wiki: Antwerp ( ; Dutch: Antwerpen [ˈɑntʋɛrpə(n)] ; French: Anvers [ɑ̃vɛʁs] ) is a city and a municipality in the Flemish Region of Belgium.

aliases: U.S. Military Academy at West Point
United States Military Academy at West Point
description: federal service academy in West Point, New York, United States
description_wiki: The United States Military Academy (USMA), commonly known as West Point, is a United States service academy in West Point, New York, that educates cadets for service as commissioned officers in the United States Army.
